summary: The Pester test suite covers the core policy logic (age, keep-latest-N, size cap) and tests policy interactions via one comprehensive combined-policies scenario. Good test organization with clear naming, helper fixtures, and deterministic reference time. However, coverage gaps include: the Run-Cleanup.ps1 CLI wrapper is not directly tested; many edge cases are missing (single artifact, all-deleted scenarios, tie-breaking, empty/zero policies, JSON parsing errors, boundary conditions); input validation tests only three failure modes instead of testing all property/type combinations; workflow structure tests verify file existence and patterns but not actual job execution. Design is strong (4/5) with maintainable code and good separation of concerns, but rigor (3/5) and coverage (3/5) are adequate rather than thorough. The test suite would catch basic regressions but might miss subtle bugs in policy interactions and error handling paths.
